response.reasoning: Step 1: Experience length — the résumé spans roughly 2006–present (~19 years including early freelance work), which meets the 15-year minimum. Step 2: Seniority — the role is a Director-level IC (Principal Engineer, AI strategy). The candidate's trajectory is Senior/Staff-level hands-on development; there is no evidence of principal-level scope, org-wide technical strategy, or advising strategic customers/partners. Step 3: AI qualifications — recent work at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow tooling, which is relevant but recent and tooling-focused; there is no recognized AI expertise, no shipped AI products or platforms at scale, and no Master's/PhD in an AI-related field. Step 4: Entrepreneurial signal — founding GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, ongoing since 2016) and workshop/conference speaking match the 'entrepreneurial experience' and 'inspire others through sharing insights' criteria. Step 5: Cloud — practical AWS/Kubernetes/CDK experience exists, but at implementation level, not platform-strategy level. Step 6: Education — Bachelor's in Computer Science satisfies the minimum. Overall: meets minimum bar on tenure and degree, has genuine builder fluency and some emerging AI-adjacent work, but falls well short of the recognized-AI-expert, director-level-strategy profile the role targets.
strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and sustains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Recent AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic-workflow tooling) plus a history of teaching and public speaking
concerns: No demonstrated director/principal-level scope — career has been senior IC delivery, not org-level technical strategy
AI experience is recent and tooling-adjacent; no recognized AI expertise or AI products/platforms shipped at scale
Cloud experience is at implementation level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), not cloud platform strategy or advising strategic customers

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. Director-level IC role
Long tenure with entrepreneurial and builder fluency

response.justification: The candidate comfortably clears the tenure and education minimums and brings genuine builder credibility, entrepreneurial initiative, and emerging AI-agent tooling experience. However, this role targets a recognized AI expert operating at director-level strategic scope — advising strategic customers, shaping cloud product futures — and the résumé shows a strong senior/staff hands-on engineer rather than a technical strategist with demonstrated AI products or platform leadership. The gap is in level and AI depth, not raw engineering ability.
